audit_items.proof_trace: In nfp_ccm_wait_reply, when wait_event_interruptible_timeout returns err<0 (signal) and skb is NULL, the code calls nfp_ccm_reply_drop_tag which may dequeue a concurrently-arrived firmware reply sk_buff; the function then returns ERR_PTR(err) at L117 without freeing this skb. The caller nfp_ccm_communicate propagates the ERR_PTR without freeing, and all higher-level callers do the same — the sk_buff is permanently leaked.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A local process interacts with the NFP NIC (e.g., via BPF map operations in cmsg.c) while the firmware responds concurrently; delivering a signal (SIGINT/SIGTERM) to that process during the 5-second wait window can trigger this race, leaking one sk_buff per occurrence and enabling kernel memory exhaustion (DoS) with repeated attempts.
